easy.companyDescription: A consumer electronics company entering a fast-growing mobile-device category.
easy.macroContext: Mobile computing is moving beyond voice and text toward richer internet services.
easy.situation: Can a premium handheld product open a larger platform before carriers and incumbents shut the window?
easy.longCase: A loyal consumer base and strong design reputation could pull users into a new category and denser ecosystem.
easy.shortCase: Carriers and entrenched device makers still control distribution, and a launch miss would be costly after a strong run-up.
setupHints: The stock already prices meaningful execution, so a weak debut leaves little cushion.

medium.companyDescription: A consumer products company selling through outside channels.
medium.macroContext: Retail partners and component suppliers are renegotiating leverage as consumer demand shifts.
medium.situation: Can a new product cycle improve sales enough to offset the cost and execution risk of changing the lineup?
medium.longCase: A well-received refresh could lift unit demand and improve the value of the existing customer base.
medium.shortCase: Inventory, supplier, and channel friction could turn a costly refresh into a margin problem.

hard.macroContext: Retail demand and supplier costs are moving unevenly across consumer categories.
hard.situation: Can a product refresh improve results before execution costs weigh on profitability?
hard.longCase: Stronger demand for an updated lineup could improve revenue and the use of existing distribution.
hard.shortCase: A delayed or expensive rollout could leave inventory and margins under pressure.

reveal.shortText: That was Apple. The iPhone launch quickly reframed the company as a mobile platform contender.
reveal.funFact: Apple sold over 6 million first-generation iPhones, but the App Store launched a year later is what truly unlocked the platform.
whyItMoved: The new handheld product gained rapid consumer attention.
Investors began pricing a broader platform opportunity.

sources.label: Prototype sample seed
sources.notes: Placeholder sample content for Phase 3 pipeline; not production research.

review.reviewNotes: H029 rewrote Medium and Hard after H028 linked design-led hardware, 2007 handheld adoption, premium brand pull, and partner distribution to Apple. Medium peer set: Apple/Sony/Motorola/Nokia. Hard peer set: Sony/Motorola/Nokia/Palm/Apple/RIM; the decision core concerns a general product-refresh execution tradeoff. Easy and shared labels remain unchanged; Medium/Hard Gate 2 results were removed as stale.

revealOnly: non-phone device base expanding into handhelds
prohibited: premium brand-pull and partner-controlled distribution conjunction
